From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Spanish film director (1929–2013)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Jos%C3%A9_Ram%C3%B3n_Larraz "Special:EditPage/José Ramón Larraz") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["José Ramón Larraz"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Ram%C3%B3n+Larraz%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Ram%C3%B3n+Larraz%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Ram%C3%B3n+Larraz%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Ram%C3%B3n+Larraz%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Ram%C3%B3n+Larraz%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Jos%C3%A9+Ram%C3%B3n+Larraz%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2013)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

In this [Spanish name](/wiki/Spanish_name "Spanish name"), the first or paternal [surname](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is  *Larraz* and the second or maternal family name is  *Gil*.

| José Ramón Larraz | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | José Ramón Larraz Gil  1929  [Barcelona](/wiki/Barcelona "Barcelona"), [Spain](/wiki/Restoration_(Spain) "Restoration (Spain)") |
| Died | 3 September 2013 (aged 84)  [Málaga](/wiki/M%C3%A1laga "Málaga"), Spain |
| Occupations | [Screenwriter](/wiki/Screenwriter "Screenwriter"), [Film director](/wiki/Film_director "Film director") and producer |
| Years active | 1970–2002 |

**José Ramón Larraz Gil**[[1]](#cite_note-1) (1929 – 3 September 2013) was a Spanish [director](/wiki/Film_director "Film director") of [exploitation](/wiki/Exploitation_film "Exploitation film") and horror films such as the erotic and bloody *[Vampyres](/wiki/Vampyres_(film) "Vampyres (film)")* (1974), *The House that Vanished* (1973), *Symptoms* (1974), *Black Candles* (1982) and *Rest in Pieces* (1987) among others.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jos%C3%A9_Ram%C3%B3n_Larraz&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

### Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jos%C3%A9_Ram%C3%B3n_Larraz&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early life")]

Born in [Barcelona](/wiki/Barcelona "Barcelona"), Larraz earned a [Doctor of Philosophy](/wiki/Doctor_of_Philosophy "Doctor of Philosophy") degree and moved to [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris") in 1952, where he started his career as a comics writer for magazines like [Pilote](/wiki/Pilote "Pilote") and [Spirou](/wiki/Spirou_(magazine) "Spirou (magazine)").[[4]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECooper2016162–163-4) His most known creation was the action-comic series "Paul Foran", which he wrote under the name "Gil" and also made some artistic contributions to.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jos%C3%A9_Ram%C3%B3n_Larraz&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Career")]

Larraz moved to [England](/wiki/England "England"), where he began making films, then in 1976 apparently [*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] relocated his operations back to Spain. He made many different types of films, but is best known for his horror films. *[Symptoms](/wiki/Symptoms_(film) "Symptoms (film)")* was an official British entry at the [1974 Cannes Film Festival](/wiki/1974_Cannes_Film_Festival "1974 Cannes Film Festival").[[6]](#cite_note-bfi-6) His last few horror films were Spanish-American co-productions.[[7]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECooper2016167-7) He apparently[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] retired from filmmaking in 1992 at age 63.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

### Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jos%C3%A9_Ram%C3%B3n_Larraz&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Death")]

Larraz died, aged 84, in [Málaga](/wiki/M%C3%A1laga "Málaga") on 3 September 2013.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Further reading

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jos%C3%A9_Ram%C3%B3n_Larraz&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Further reading")]

* The book *[Immoral Tales: European Sex & Horror Movies 1956-1984](/wiki/Immoral_Tales:_European_Sex_%26_Horror_Movies_1956-1984 "Immoral Tales: European Sex & Horror Movies 1956-1984")* (1994) by Cathal Tohill and Pete Tombs dedicates a chapter to him.
* The paperback novelisation of *[Vampyres](/wiki/Vampyres "Vampyres")* (FAB Press, 2001) by Tim Greaves.
* *Vampyres: A Tribute to the Ultimate in Erotic Horror Cinema* (1996) by Tim Greaves is devoted entirely to the making of the film, filled with interview material, reviews and a treasure trove of photos.
